book.title: The Aesop for Children
book.source: Aesop, ill. Milo Winter (Rand McNally, 1919). Public domain in the USA. Text via Project Gutenberg #19994.

level.title: The Fox and the Grapes
level.moral: There are many who pretend to despise and belittle that which is beyond their reach.

segments.value: trained along the branches of a tree. The grapes seemed ready to burst with juice, and the Fox's mouth watered as he gazed

at them.

The bunch hung from a
branch, and the Fox had to jump for it. The first time he jumped he
it by a long way. So he walked off a short

and took a running leap at it, only to fall short once more. Again and again he tried, but in vain.

Now he sat down and looked at the grapes in disgust.

"What a
I am," he said. "Here I am wearing myself out to get a bunch of

grapes that are not worth gaping for."

And off he walked very, very
